# SKILL.md — Motion & UI Implementation Guide
# Read this before generating any animated component.
# Generated by: npx motion-md init

---

## Purpose

This file makes you immediately more capable of producing consistent,
professional, physics-aware UI motion. MOTION.md contains the actual
values for this project. This file contains the principles.

**Rule zero:** import motionTokens from @/lib/motion-tokens for all
animation values. Never hardcode duration or cubic-bezier strings.

---

## Core Motion Principles

### 1. Timing communicates weight
Fast = light, weightless, snappy. Slow = heavy, deliberate, important.
A button press at 80ms feels mechanical. A modal at 80ms feels broken.
Match duration to the perceived mass of the element.

### 2. Easing communicates physics
- **Decelerate** `cubic-bezier(0.05,0.7,0.1,1)` — element enters, arrives, settles.
  Use for: enters, modals sliding in, drawers opening.
- **Standard** `cubic-bezier(0.2,0,0,1)` — natural, balanced movement.
  Use for: page transitions, hover states, general transitions.
- **Accelerate** `cubic-bezier(0.3,0,0.8,0.15)` — element exits, leaves with energy.
  Use for: exits, dismissals, elements leaving the screen.
- **Spring** `cubic-bezier(0.34,1.56,0.64,1)` — overshoots, feels alive.
  Use for: press feedback, playful enters, toggles, celebrate moments.
- **Linear** — no easing, mechanical.
  Use for: shake animations only. Never for enters or exits.

### 3. Asymmetry — exits are always faster
Enter: full duration from MOTION.md.
Exit: 50–65% of the enter duration. Always.
A drawer that takes 400ms to open should take 200–250ms to close.
Users don't wait for things to leave — they wait for things to arrive.

### 4. Staging — one thing leads
When multiple elements animate, one leads and others follow.
The modal overlay fades in first, then the dialog scales up.
The list container enters, then each item staggers.
Never animate everything simultaneously at equal weight.

### 5. Distance matches scale
Small elements: 4–8px travel distance.
Medium elements (cards, panels): 16–24px.
Large elements (modals, drawers): 24–48px.
Full-screen transitions: translate the full axis.

---

## Component Implementation Patterns

### Drawer (slides from right)
```tsx
import { motion, AnimatePresence } from 'framer-motion'
import { motionTokens } from '@/lib/motion-tokens'

<AnimatePresence>
  {open && (
    <motion.div
      initial={{ x: '100%' }}
      animate={{ x: 0, transition: {
        duration: motionTokens.drawer.duration,
        ease: motionTokens.drawer.ease,
      }}}
      exit={{ x: '100%', transition: {
        duration: motionTokens.drawer.duration * 0.6,
      }}}
    >
      {children}
    </motion.div>
  )}
</AnimatePresence>
```

### Stagger list
```tsx
const dist = motionTokens.stagger.itemDistancePx ?? 12
const cascade = motionTokens.stagger.direction === 'cascade'
const container = {
  animate: { transition: { staggerChildren: motionTokens.stagger.staggerDelay } }
}
const item = {
  initial: cascade
    ? { opacity: 0, y: dist }
    : { opacity: 0, x: -dist },
  animate: cascade
    ? { opacity: 1, y: 0, transition: {
        duration: motionTokens.stagger.itemDuration,
        ease: motionTokens.stagger.ease,
      }}
    : { opacity: 1, x: 0, transition: {
        duration: motionTokens.stagger.itemDuration,
        ease: motionTokens.stagger.ease,
      }},
}

<motion.ul variants={container} initial="initial" animate="animate">
  {items.map(i => <motion.li key={i.id} variants={item}>{i.label}</motion.li>)}
</motion.ul>
```

**even** — items offset horizontally (`x: -distance`), fade in.
**cascade** — items offset vertically (`y: +distance`), fade in.
Set in MOTION.md under `stagger.direction` and `stagger.item-distance`.

### Button press
```tsx
<motion.button
  whileTap={{ scale: motionTokens.press.scale }}
  transition={{ duration: motionTokens.press.duration, ease: motionTokens.press.ease }}
>
  Click me
</motion.button>
```

---

## Anti-Patterns — Never do these

1. **Hardcoding values** — `duration: 0.3` instead of `motionTokens.enter.duration`.
   Why wrong: breaks consistency, impossible to update system-wide.
   Fix: always import motionTokens.

2. **Linear easing on enters** — `ease: 'linear'` for an element appearing.
   Why wrong: feels mechanical and cheap. Linear = shake animations only.
   Fix: use decelerate for enters, spring for playful.

3. **Symmetric enter/exit timing** — same duration for open and close.
   Why wrong: exits feel sluggish. Users don't wait for things to leave.
   Fix: exit at 50–65% of enter duration.

4. **Animating layout properties** — `width`, `height`, `top`, `left`.
   Why wrong: triggers layout recalculation, causes jank on every frame.
   Fix: use transform (translateX, translateY, scale) and opacity only.

5. **Simultaneous equal-weight animation** — everything animates at once.
   Why wrong: visual noise, no focal point, feels chaotic.
   Fix: stage animations — one leads, others follow with stagger.

6. **Missing AnimatePresence for exits** — removing elements without exit animation.
   Why wrong: elements pop out with no transition.
   Fix: always wrap conditionally-rendered animated elements in AnimatePresence.

7. **Ignoring prefers-reduced-motion** — no reduced motion fallback.
   Why wrong: accessibility failure, causes issues for users with vestibular disorders.
   Fix: see Accessibility section below.

---

## Accessibility

Always handle `prefers-reduced-motion`. Two approaches:

### CSS (global, in globals.css)
```css
@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {
  :root {
    --motion-enter-duration: 1ms;
    --motion-drawer-duration: 1ms;
    --motion-press-duration: 1ms;
    --motion-stagger-duration: 1ms;
    --motion-stagger-delay: 0ms;
  }
}
```

### Framer Motion (per component)
```tsx
const reduceMotion = useReducedMotion()

<motion.div
  animate={{ opacity: 1, y: 0 }}
  transition={{
    duration: reduceMotion ? 0.15 : motionTokens.enter.duration,
    ease: motionTokens.enter.ease,
  }}
/>
```

Do not collapse to 0ms — a tiny 0.15s opacity fade is acceptable and
preserves the sense of state change without causing vestibular issues.

---

## Pre-ship Checklist

Before shipping any animated component, answer yes to all:

- [ ] Did I import motionTokens instead of hardcoding any value?
- [ ] Does the enter animation use decelerate or spring easing (not linear)?
- [ ] Is the exit duration shorter than the enter duration?
- [ ] Did I wrap conditional renders in AnimatePresence for exit animations?
- [ ] Does transform handle the movement (not top/left/width/height)?
- [ ] Does the stagger have consistent duration — only the delay varies?
- [ ] Did I test with prefers-reduced-motion: reduce enabled?
- [ ] Does the motion feel appropriate for the element's perceived mass?
- [ ] After editing MOTION.md, did I run npm run sync:motion?

---

*MOTION.md is the source of truth for values. This file is principles.*
*If MOTION.md and this file conflict, MOTION.md wins.*
